sprint: Lumo Voice — First Real Recording Wired In (Tap-Grid Gatekeeper Line)
task: Begins replacing Lumo's typewriter-only dialogue with real recorded voice lines, one at a time, as the user supplies them. New js/lumoVoice.js is a small, ID-keyed audio lookup mirroring js/gatewayAudio.js's own proven discipline exactly: no autoplay, playback fires only from inside a real click-originated call chain, cached Audio objects, every failure swallowed defensively so a missing/unrecorded line is a normal, silent no-op -- the on-screen text still reveals via its own typewriter animation with no audio, exactly as before any recording existed. The Creator Signature tap-grid gatekeeper's own greeting line (js/magicCardUI.js's _buildGatekeeperHeader) is the first to get real audio: its text collapsed from a nickname/fallback ternary ("<name>, the Creator -- show me your stars, and you may pass." / "Traveler, show me your stars, and you may pass.") into one unified line, "My friend, show me your stars, and you may pass.", per explicit direction -- no nickname/fallback split needed once the greeting itself doesn't address anyone by name. LumoVoice.play('tapgrid') fires alongside the typewriter reveal, playing the real assets/lumo/voice/lumo-01-tapgrid.mp3 recording. Verified live (not just the isolated audio file) via a headless-browser check driving the actual reachable code path -- MagicCardUI.checkIdentityGate()'s standalone Welcome screen's "Return to My Adventure" button, since the Traveller Gateway's own primary Returning-Creator boot flow (beginCreatorSignature) still deliberately passes skipSpeech:true, to avoid repeating the Gateway's own separate "Welcome home... show me your stars" greeting -- confirming the correct unified text renders and a real Audio().play() call fires against the correct file. Full regression across the entire existing Gateway/Magic-Card Playwright suite (gw_returning_unified_test.js, tapgrid_visibility_check.js) passes unchanged, zero console errors. The remaining ~11 Lumo lines (Gateway greeting/reunion, Creator Ceremony beats) stay on typewriter-only text pending their own recordings, per the user's own explicit "we will update the text prompts in code in one go" instruction for the rest of the batch. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to a new js/lumoVoice.js, plus js/magicCardUI.js and index.html.
